buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Feature matrix and target series, both indexed by date
features = pd.DataFrame({
    'momentum': [0.01, -0.02, 0.03, 0.01, -0.01],
    'volume': [1000, 1200, 800, 1100, 900]
}, index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5))

target = pd.Series(
    [0.02, -0.01, 0.04, 0.00, -0.02],
    index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5)
)

# Sort features by momentum score for ranking
features_sorted = features.sort_values('momentum', ascending=False)

# Fit model using sorted features with ORIGINAL target
X = features_sorted.values
y = target.values  # BUG: target not reindexed to match sorted features

model.fit(X, y)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

features = pd.DataFrame({
    'momentum': [0.01, -0.02, 0.03, 0.01, -0.01],
    'volume': [1000, 1200, 800, 1100, 900]
}, index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5))

target = pd.Series(
    [0.02, -0.01, 0.04, 0.00, -0.02],
    index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5)
)

# Sort features by momentum
features_sorted = features.sort_values('momentum', ascending=False)

# ALWAYS realign target to match the sorted index
target_aligned = target.loc[features_sorted.index]

X = features_sorted.values
y = target_aligned.values  # Now correctly aligned

model.fit(X, y)
description: features are sorted by a column but target is extracted from original unsorted order.
explanation: After sort_values(), features_sorted has rows in a different order than the original. But target.values extracts the target in the original date order, not the sorted order. X[0] is now the row with highest momentum, but y[0] is still the return from 2020-01-01 — completely different observations. The model trains on mismatched (feature, label) pairs, silently producing a model with random predictive power.
suggested_fix: After any sort operation, always realign all arrays using the sorted index: target_aligned = target.loc[features_sorted.index]

issues.title: Target not realigned after sort_values causes feature-label mismatch
